perturbation.original_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder for Fazync. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its best efforts to provide the following services to Fazync a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide. b. Provide a quarterly Pipeline or project information leads report to Fazync a monthly basis which contains a 3-month rolling forecast of potential sales.
perturbation.changed_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder for Fazync. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its reasonable endeavors to provide the following services to Fazync a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide. b. Provide a quarterly Pipeline or project information leads report to Fazync a monthly basis which contains a 3-month rolling forecast of potential sales.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'best efforts' to 'reasonable endeavors' introduces a contradiction. 'Best efforts' implies a higher standard of diligence and commitment than 'reasonable endeavors'. This creates ambiguity as to the level of effort Bravatek is actually obligated to exert. This contradicts the expectations set for Bravatek in the rest of the contract, where more stringent duties are implied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder for Fazync. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its reasonable endeavors to provide the following services to Fazync a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide. b. Provide a quarterly Pipeline or project information leads report to Fazync a monthly basis which contains a 3-month rolling forecast of potential sales. c. Follow-up on on-going project leads that Fazync actively engaged with or believes is appropriate. d. Provide Fazync with any promotional materials, technical papers, white papers, proposals, etc. prior to publication or delivery to prospective clients.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'best efforts' to 'reasonable endeavors' may represent a change in the standard of care required of Bravatek, but it doesn't create a direct and undeniable contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text lists Bravatek's duties, and while 'reasonable endeavors' might be interpreted as requiring less effort than 'best efforts' in performing those duties, it doesn't prevent Bravatek from fulfilling them. A reader could reasonably interpret the contract as a whole to mean Bravatek must exert reasonable effort in all listed responsibilities. Therefore, the change does not meet the threshold for a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a finder's fee, to be unilaterally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 5% to maximum of 10% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 60 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.explanation: The changes in this section significantly alter the compensation terms for Bravatek. Replacing 'lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by Fazync' with 'finder's fee, to be unilaterally decided by Fazync' shifts the power dynamic and contradicts the initial agreement of mutual benefit. Also changing the range of fees from 10-20% to 5-10% directly impacts Bravatek's potential earnings. Furthermore, the payment terms have been altered from NET 30 to NET 60, increasing the time Bravatek must wait to receive payment. These adjustments cause a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding the promised compensation to Bravatek.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a finder's fee, to be unilaterally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 5% to maximum of 10% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 60 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are identical. Given that the 'Perturbation Explanation' details a significant shift in compensation terms, this strongly implies that the change introduces a contradiction. It alters Bravatek's compensation by changing 'mutually discussed' to 'unilaterally decided,' reducing the fee range (10-20% to 5-10%), and extending the payment timeframe (NET 30 to NET 60). This directly conflicts with the initial understanding and therefore creates an in-text contradiction. However, the Perturbation type "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction" means that the contradiction has to be derived by terms being used interchangeably without being defined, and leading to conflicting obligations. This is not the case here. Therefore, according to the given Perturbation Definitions, I must assign a 'NO'.

perturbation.original_text: Fazync shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of Fazync or breach of Fazync of any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Fazync shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for direct damages only, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred specifically as a direct result of third-party claim, action or proceeding arising solely from the intentional misconduct of Fazync directly related to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides broad indemnification for any damages related to Fazync's actions, including negligence. The revised text limits the indemnification to 'direct damages only' resulting from 'intentional misconduct' directly related to the agreement, excluding damages due to negligence. This contradicts the broad indemnity initially granted, potentially leaving Bravatek unprotected against claims arising from Fazync's negligence. The exclusion of 'negligence' narrows the scope of indemnification and thus creates an in-text contradiction regarding the extent of liability Fazync is willing to cover.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fazync shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for direct damages only, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred specifically as a direct result of third-party claim, action or proceeding arising solely from the intentional misconduct of Fazync directly related to this Agreement. Bravatek shall indemnify Fazync, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs, incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of Bravatek or breach of Bravatek of any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for indemnification for damages arising from negligence, while the changed text limits indemnification to direct damages resulting solely from intentional misconduct directly related to the agreement. The contradicted text contains both statements. Thus, the changed text directly contradicts the portion of the contradicted text that allows for indemnification of damages arising from negligence. This constitutes a clear in-text contradiction.
